title: Play with me t_t
content: June 3, 2025  Today after work I suddenly felt like playing some Minecraft. It’s been a while since I last logged in, and for some reason the idea just popped into my head while I was on the way home.  Playing alone can get a bit boring sometimes, so I messaged 2hi5hu and asked if he wanted to join later in the evening. At first he said “maybe”, which I took as a yes. I spent a little time preparing a small area in the world and waited for him to show up.  Well… he never did.  By the time it got late there was still no sign of him online. I guess he must have gotten busy with something else or simply forgot. In the end I just explored the world alone for a while, mining a bit and walking around the base.  At some point I had a random idea and built a tiny “memorial” near the spawn point with a sign that simply said: “Here lies 2hi5hu, who promised to play but never arrived.”  It was mostly a joke, but it did make the empty world feel a little less quiet. Maybe next time he’ll actually show up.  —  2025年6月3日  今天下班之后突然有点想玩 Minecraft。好像很久没有上线了，在回家的路上不知道为什么突然就想起来了。  一个人玩有时候会有点无聊，于是就顺手问了一下 2hi5hu 晚上要不要一起上线。他当时回了个“可能吧”，我就当他是答应了。于是我先上服务器稍微整理了一下附近的区域，等他上线。  结果等到晚上挺晚了，人影都没见到。  大概是临时有别的事情，或者干脆忘了吧。最后只能自己一个人随便逛了逛世界，挖了点矿，在基地附近转了转。  后来突然有个很无聊的想法，就在出生点旁边简单堆了个小“墓碑”，放了个牌子写着：“这里埋葬着 2hi5hu —— 说好要来玩但从没出现的人。”  虽然只是开个玩笑，但多少让这个空荡荡的世界看起来没那么冷清了。希望下次他真的能上线吧。

title: Happy birthday
content: Nov 23, 2024  Today is my birthday. I didn’t really plan to celebrate it in any special way—just another normal day. When I woke up this morning, I noticed a few messages from friends wishing me a happy birthday. It was a small thing, but it definitely made the morning feel a bit warmer.  Work was pretty normal. A couple of coworkers found out it was my birthday and insisted on getting lunch together. Nothing fancy, just a simple meal and some casual conversation. They joked about me getting older, which I guess is unavoidable every year.  After work I headed home earlier than usual. I stopped by a small bakery on the way and bought a slice of cake for myself. It felt a little funny celebrating alone, but at the same time it was actually quite relaxing. Sometimes a quiet evening is exactly what you need.  Later at night I spent some time browsing a few tech blogs and organizing some notes I’ve been writing recently. I’ve been trying to keep things documented more regularly—ideas, small projects, random thoughts.  Birthdays used to feel like a big event when I was younger. These days they’re a lot simpler, but maybe that’s not a bad thing. A calm day, a few messages from friends, and some time to myself feels like a pretty decent way to mark another year.  — 2024年11月23日  今天是我的生日，其实没有打算特别庆祝，只是普通的一天。早上醒来时看到朋友发来的几条生日祝福消息，虽然只是简单的问候，但还是让人感觉心情很好。  白天工作和平时差不多，有同事知道是我生日，就一起出去简单吃了顿午饭。大家一边聊天一边开玩笑说我又老了一岁，不过气氛倒是挺轻松的。  下班后我比平时早一点回家，路过一家小面包店的时候顺便买了一块小蛋糕。一个人简单吃点东西，反而感觉挺放松的。  晚上花了一点时间看看技术博客，也整理了一下最近写的笔记。最近在尝试把一些想法和项目记录下来，感觉慢慢养成这个习惯还挺不错的。  小时候的生日总觉得很隆重，现在反而变得简单很多。不过这样平静的一天，其实也挺好的。

title: How they found me??
content: September 2, 2024  Something a little weird happened today and it’s been bothering me for most of the evening.  Recently I started noticing a few random people mentioning one of my old usernames in messages and comments. It’s not a name I’ve used for a long time, so seeing it suddenly appear again felt pretty strange. I honestly can’t remember where it might have been exposed.  What surprised me even more was that someone actually managed to find my account on another platform and sent me a message there. It wasn’t anything harmful, just a random comment about recognizing the name, but it still felt a bit unsettling.  I keep wondering how they even connected everything together. Maybe it was from some old screenshots, or a forgotten profile somewhere that still had the same name. The internet has a long memory, apparently.  Anyway, I spent a bit of time tonight checking some of my old accounts and settings. It might be a good idea to clean up a few things and remove information that’s no longer necessary. Sometimes you forget how many traces you leave online until something like this reminds you.  —  2024年9月2日  今天发生了一件有点奇怪的事情，晚上一直在想这件事。  最近突然有几个人在留言或者私信里提到我以前用过的一个旧网名。那个名字其实已经很久没用了，所以突然看到有人提起来的时候还是有点意外。  更离谱的是，居然还有人根据这个名字在另一个平台找到了我的账号，然后给我发了消息。虽然内容也只是随便说认出了这个名字，但还是让人感觉有点不太舒服。  我一直在想他们到底是怎么把这些信息联系起来的。可能是某些很久以前的截图，也可能是某个忘记改掉名字的旧账号。互联网有时候真的记得太多东西了。  晚上花了一点时间检查了一下自己的一些旧账号和资料，感觉确实该清理一下了。有时候不出这种事情，都不会意识到自己在网上留下了多少痕迹。

title: Don't spam
content: August 9, 2024  Something a bit strange happened today. When I checked my inbox this morning, I noticed a bunch of emails from people I didn’t recognize. Most of them looked like random messages or advertisements, and a few were clearly spam.  At first I thought it was just the usual junk mail that slips through filters, but the number of messages kept increasing throughout the day. It made me wonder if my email address somehow ended up on some mailing list or got leaked somewhere.  I spent a little time going through the inbox and marking the obvious ones as spam. Hopefully the filters will get better after that. It’s a bit annoying though—email used to feel much quieter.  For now I might just ignore most of them and see if things calm down in a few days. If it keeps happening, I may have to update some of my account settings or switch to a different address for important stuff.  —  2024年8月9日  今天遇到一件有点奇怪的事情。早上查看邮箱的时候，发现收件箱里多了不少陌生人的邮件。大多数看起来像是一些广告或者完全不认识的人发来的内容，有几封明显就是垃圾邮件。  一开始还以为只是平时偶尔会出现的垃圾邮件，但到了下午数量好像越来越多了。这让我有点怀疑自己的邮箱是不是被某个地方泄露了，或者被加入了什么奇怪的邮件列表。  后来花了一点时间把那些明显的垃圾邮件都标记了一下，希望邮箱的过滤系统之后能自动拦截掉一些。  虽然不是什么严重的问题，但突然多出这么多莫名其妙的邮件还是挺烦的。先观察几天看看情况，如果还是这样，可能得考虑换一个邮箱来处理比较重要的事情了。

title: Normal life
content: July 6, 2024  Work was actually pretty interesting today. In the afternoon I spent some time discussing a small technical problem with my colleague shukuang. I’ve always felt that he’s one of those people who can understand complicated systems incredibly quickly.  We were looking at a strange bug that had been bothering me for a while. I had already tried a few different approaches but couldn’t quite figure out what was going wrong. After I showed him the code and explained the situation, he spent a few minutes thinking about it, asked a couple of questions, and then immediately pointed out where the issue probably was.  Turns out he was right. After adjusting a small part of the logic, the whole thing started working normally again.  Moments like that always remind me how impressive some people’s technical intuition can be. He’s definitely one of the strongest engineers on the team, and honestly I still have a lot to learn.  We chatted a bit afterward and the conversation drifted to random daily life topics. I remember he mentioned that his partner, kanna.seto, recently started learning some new things as well, which sounded pretty interesting.  Days like this make work feel a little more enjoyable. Even when you run into problems, it’s nice to have people around who are genuinely good at what they do.  —  2024年7月6日  今天工作还挺有意思的。下午和同事 shukuang 一起讨论了一个技术问题。我一直觉得他是那种对复杂系统理解特别快的人。  之前有个奇怪的 bug 困扰我好一阵了，自己试了好几种方法都没完全搞明白。后来把代码给他看了一下，大概讲了一下情况。他想了几分钟，又问了几个问题，很快就指出了可能出问题的地方。  结果还真是那里。稍微调整了一点逻辑之后，程序就恢复正常了。  这种时候就会很明显感觉到，有些人的技术直觉确实很厉害。他算是团队里技术特别强的那种工程师，我自己其实还有很多东西要学。  后来又随便聊了一会儿，话题慢慢就聊到一些生活上的事情。他提到自己的对象叫 kanna.seto，好像最近也在学习一些新的东西。  感觉有时候工作里遇到问题也不完全是坏事，至少能从别人身上学到不少东西。

title: Sad
content: May 14, 2024  Today wasn’t a particularly great day. In the afternoon I got called into a short meeting by my supervisor about a small mistake in a report I submitted earlier this week. It wasn’t anything serious, but I still ended up getting a bit of a lecture about being more careful with details.  I guess it’s part of work life, but it still left me feeling a little frustrated for the rest of the day. Sometimes it’s strange how one small conversation can affect your mood for hours.  After getting home I didn’t really feel like doing much. I made some simple dinner, watched a few random videos, and tried not to think too much about work. At some point I opened my profile page and randomly decided to change my avatar. The old one had been there for quite a while anyway, so maybe it was time for something different.  It’s funny how small changes like that can slightly improve your mood. Nothing big, just a different picture and a fresh look. Hopefully tomorrow will be a better day.  —  2024年5月14日  今天其实不算太顺利。下午被上级叫去聊了一下，说是之前提交的一份报告里有些细节处理得不够仔细。虽然事情不算严重，但还是被提醒要更加注意。  这种事情其实在工作里很正常，不过当时还是有点不太开心。感觉一天的心情多少被影响了一些。  下班回家之后也没什么特别想做的事情，随便做了点吃的，看了会儿视频，尽量不去想工作的事情。后来打开自己的账号页面，突然就想着把头像换掉了。之前那个头像其实已经用了挺久，正好换个新的。  有时候这种很小的改变，反而会让人感觉轻松一点。希望明天能顺利一点吧。

title: Today
content: April 18, 2024  Work wasn’t very busy today. I finished a few small tasks in the morning, and in the afternoon I mostly spent time reading some tech blogs and organizing my own notes. Recently I’ve been trying to write down some scattered ideas on my blog. It helps me think more clearly when everything is recorded.  When I got home after work, Momo was already waiting near the door like usual. Momo is a Ragdoll cat, very gentle and extremely affectionate. As soon as I put my laptop bag down, it jumped onto the desk and started purring, almost like it was asking me to play with it.  Later in the evening I brushed Momo’s fur and gave it some canned food. After eating, it just lay lazily near the window watching the lights outside. Sometimes I feel that having a cat is surprisingly comforting, even when we’re just quietly staying in the same room.  Hopefully I’ll have more time this weekend to spend with Momo, and maybe organize some of the code and blog drafts I’ve been working on.  ——— 2024年4月18日  今天的工作其实不算太忙。上午在公司把几个小问题处理完之后，下午基本都在看一些技术博客和整理自己的笔记。最近在尝试把一些零散的想法写到博客里，感觉这样记录下来会更清晰一点。  下班回到家之后，Momo 又像往常一样跑到门口等我。Momo 是一只布偶猫（Ragdoll cat），性格特别温顺，也很黏人。我刚把电脑包放下，它就跳到桌子旁边，一边打呼噜一边看着我，好像在催我陪它玩。  晚上给 Momo 梳了梳毛，又喂了一点猫罐头。它吃完以后就懒洋洋地躺在窗边发呆，看着外面的灯光。我有时候会觉得，养一只猫其实挺治愈的，哪怕只是安静地待在同一个房间里。  希望周末能有时间多陪陪它，也顺便整理一下最近写的代码和博客内容。
